content.text:
Now I can see the issue clearly. In `TextBox.cs` line 154, `enableDataValidation: true` is set, but in `ComboBox.cs` line 88-89, this flag is not set.

Let me also look at how the `AddOwner` method works with `enableDataValidation`:

content.content: No matches found

content.text: Now I understand the fix needed. The `ComboBox.TextProperty` needs `enableDataValidation: true` added to its metadata. Let me look at the `AvaloniaPropertyMetadata` and `StyledPropertyMetadata` to understand how `enableDataValidation` gets passed through `AddOwner`:
Now let me look at how the metadata from `TextBlock.TextProperty` is structured to understand what `AddOwner` with new metadata would need:

Now I have a clear understanding of the issue. The fix is straightforward: in `ComboBox.cs` line 88-89, add `enableDataValidation: true` to the `AddOwner` metadata.

Let me also check for existing tests related to ComboBox data

content.content: No files found

content.text: Let me check existing tests for ComboBox:

content.content:
tests/Avalonia.IntegrationTests.Appium/ComboBoxTests.cs
tests/Avalonia.Controls.UnitTests/ComboBoxTests.cs

content.text: Now I have a clear understanding. Let me now look at how data validation works with properties to understand the expected behavior, and then check the `AvaloniaPropertyMetadata.Merge` to ensure the metadata override through `AddOwner` will properly set `enableDataValidation`

I see an important detail on line 73: `EnableDataValidation ??= baseMetadata.EnableDataValidation;` — this means when `EnableDataValidation` is `null` (not set), it will inherit from the base metadata. However, let me check what the `StyledPropertyMetadata` constructor passes to the base when creating the metadata for `AddOwner`:

Looking at line 25-26 of `StyledPropertyMetadata`1.cs`: `bool enableDataValidation = false) : base(defaultBindingMode, enableDataValidation)` — the default is `false`, not `null`. On line 73 of `AvaloniaPropertyMetadata.cs`, `EnableDataValidation ??= baseMetadata.EnableDataValidation` — since `false` is not `null`, the `??=` won't assign from the base. 

So when `AddOwner` is called with `enableDataValidation` explicitly set to `true`, it will override. Let me verify: the `ComboBox.cs` line 89 currently has:
```csharp
TextBlock.TextProperty.AddOwner<ComboBox>(new(string.Empty, BindingMode.TwoWay));
```

This creates a `StyledPropertyMetadata<string?>` with `enableDataValidation: false` (the default), which means data validation won't work. The fix is to add `enableDataValidation: true`.

Let me now make the fix:

input.old_string:
public static readonly StyledProperty<string?> TextProperty =
            TextBlock.TextProperty.AddOwner<ComboBox>(new(string.Empty, BindingMode.TwoWay));
input.new_string:
public static readonly StyledProperty<string?> TextProperty =
            TextBlock.TextProperty.AddOwner<ComboBox>(new(string.Empty, BindingMode.TwoWay, enableDataValidation: true));

content.content: The file /testbed/src/Avalonia.Controls/ComboBox.cs has been updated successfully.

tool_use_result.oldString:
public static readonly StyledProperty<string?> TextProperty =
            TextBlock.TextProperty.AddOwner<ComboBox>(new(string.Empty, BindingMode.TwoWay));
tool_use_result.newString:
public static readonly StyledProperty<string?> TextProperty =
            TextBlock.TextProperty.AddOwner<ComboBox>(new(string.Empty, BindingMode.TwoWay, enableDataValidation: true));
